symptoms.symptom_name: Severe post-prandial cramps, distension and explosive diarrhoea with flushing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid post-food cramps, distension, diarrhoea and flushing fit gut mast cell degranulation.
Post-food GI symptoms with flushing can be part of anaphylaxis if circulatory/airway features are present.
Postural faintness with tachycardia indicates significant fluid loss.
Near-syncope can reflect an anaphylactic circulatory component after a food trigger.

symptoms.symptom_name: No airway involvement (no throat tightness/voice change/wheeze)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of airway/breathing features lowers, but given GI+flushing+near-syncope does not fully exclude, an anaphylactic process.

symptoms.symptom_name: Soft (not rigid) abdomen, no blood in stool, pain eases after motions

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No peritonism, no GI bleeding, and pain relief after motions argue against a surgical abdomen, though it needs in-person confirmation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Ongoing large fluid loss with reduced intake, living alone

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Heavy losses with poor intake and isolation increase the risk of collapse and AKI.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reaction reproducibly triggered by specific foods

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reproducible food triggers support mast cell food reactivity.

symptoms.symptom_name: Distended tympanic abdomen, hyperactive bowel sounds, soft on palpation, no guarding/rebound (examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hypermotility with a soft, non-peritonitic abdomen fits a functional gut flare.
No guarding/rebound argues against peritonitis, but re-examination is needed.
Known MCAS with food triggers supports a gut mast cell flare and flags anaphylaxis risk.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: GI mast cell activation flare with dehydration
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: A severe post-food GI mast cell flare with flushing, heavy fluid loss, tachycardia and near-syncope in an isolated patient needs urgent assessment for IV rehydration and to exclude a surgical abdomen — and the food-triggered flushing/GI/near-syncope combination means anaphylaxis must be screened and an adrenaline plan ensured.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person abdominal assessment and vitals/postural BP
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude a surgical abdomen and quantify dehydration; cannot be done via chat.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: IV rehydration and bloods (U&E, FBC, CRP, lactate)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To correct volume depletion and screen for ischaemia/infection/renal impairment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum tryptase (if seen acutely) and allergy/immunology review
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To assess for an anaphylactic/mast cell component and plan management.
